matches.rationale: The Bash rule triggers when creating a pull request and its evidence requires the library-local pre-PR commands; among the three command-specific gold rules, this is a valid but equally broad match, so the 1:1 constraint assigns it only to g1.
The Edit rule targets double-backtick content in source files and cites the prohibition on Sphinx-style double backticks in docstrings and comments, matching the prohibited edit in g5.

misses.note: The lint obligation is a separate bullet in the pre-PR command list, but the compiler collapsed the list into one broad extracted rule, which is already consumed by the closest available 1:1 assignment.

The test obligation is a separate bullet in the pre-PR command list, but the compiler collapsed the list into one broad extracted rule, which is already consumed by the closest available 1:1 assignment.
The compiler deliberately self-reported this as uncompilable: "TEST environment variable syntax for running a specific test file — usage documentation, no obligation/prohibition attached".

The compiler combined the positive single-backtick guidance with the double-backtick prohibition, but its actual trigger matches only double-backtick text; that extracted rule is therefore matched to g5 and cannot also satisfy g6 under the 1:1 constraint.
